markdownRemark.html:
Technology Trend Analysis — Daily Intelligence Briefing

Date: 2026-05-09 | Classification: Market Intelligence | Alert Level: Moderate — AI coding agent tools continue dominating GitHub, app store paid charts show stability

Overview

The AI coding agent ecosystem continues to consolidate its grip on developer attention, with agentic skills repositories, DeepSeek-powered terminal agents, and agent orchestration frameworks occupying 8 of 10 Trendshift top spots. On the revenue side, Stan holds steady at $3.57M MRR while TrimRx's 27% growth rate remains the strongest signal in the SaaS marketplace. Apple's paid app charts across Mac and iOS reveal a remarkably stable landscape dominated by utility stalwarts — Shadowrocket tops both platforms — while Safari extensions and privacy tools continue their quiet revenue march.

Methodology

Data collected May 9, 2026 from Trendshift (GitHub trending), TrustMRR (SaaS revenue), Mac App Store top paid chart, and iOS App Store top paid chart. Hacker News was unavailable due to automated request blocking. Android Google Play data was unavailable (AppBrain Cloudflare block, no browser budget in this micro-agent architecture). Rankings reflect real-time positions at time of collection.

GitHub Trending — AI Agent Dominance

Rank Project Stars Category
1 Production-grade engineering skills for AI coding agents 35.3K AI coding skills
2 Coding agent for DeepSeek models (terminal) 13.3K AI dev tools
3 Agent framework / software development methodology 21K Agent orchestration
4 DeepSeek (main repo) 119.8K AI models
5 CLAUDE.md file (Karpathy-inspired) 65.7K Prompt engineering
6 Review-first terminal diff viewer for agentic coders 2.7K AI dev tools
7 Skills for Real Engineers (.claude directory) 182.2K AI coding skills
8 OpenReel Video (open-source CapCut alternative) 1.7K Video editing
9 Build Agents from Scratch (从零开始构建智能体) 43.9K AI education
10 Open-Source Multimodal AI Agent Stack 30.2K Agent frameworks

The pattern is unmistakable: 8 of 10 trending repos serve the AI coding agent workflow. The .claude directory ecosystem alone accounts for two entries (ranks 1 and 7, combined 217K+ stars), signaling that prompt engineering and agent skill definitions have become a first-class development concern. DeepSeek's main repo at 119.8K stars and its terminal coding agent at 13.3K demonstrate that the Chinese AI lab has built genuine developer mindshare. The only non-AI entry is OpenReel Video, an open-source browser-based video editor positioning itself as a CapCut alternative — notable as a signal that creator tooling remains a viable independent category.

SaaS Revenue — TrustMRR Top 10

Rank Company MRR Growth
1 Stan $3,569,654 —
2 Unnamed Company $378,825 5%
3 Rezi $295,056 4%
4 TrimRx $245,940 27%
5 1Lookup $238,758 13%
6 Kibu $234,319 —
7 Cometly $206,891 1%
8 Supliful $195,884 3%
9 DM Champ $188,411 1%
10 Stealth Venture (Simon) $187,054 4%

Stan's $3.57M MRR continues to dwarf the field — the creator economy marketplace is a revenue category unto itself. The real story is TrimRx at 27% growth, the telehealth GLP-1 weight loss platform riding the Ozempic wave with digital-native distribution. Among mid-tier SaaS, 1Lookup's 13% growth in data validation APIs and Cometly's flat 1% in marketing attribution reflect a broader pattern: infrastructure and compliance tools outperform marketing tools in the current cycle.

19 RadarScope Weather
20 RAR Extractor Archive Utility
21 Tampermonkey Safari Extension
22 Mainstage Music Production
23 SiteSucker Web Download
24 Scrivener 3 Writing
25 LibreOffice Office Suite

The Mac paid chart is a monument to focused utility. VPN tools (Shadowrocket), archive managers (Keka, RAR Extractor), disk analyzers (DaisyDisk, GrandPerspective), and Safari extensions (Wipr 2, Noir, Dark Reader, Tampermonkey) collectively account for 14 of 25 spots. The chart has barely moved since yesterday — Shadowrocket, Logic Pro, and Keka hold the same top 3. This stability signals a mature market where incumbents are extremely hard to displace. Notable new entry: Scrivener 3 at rank 24, the long-form writing tool making a reappearance, and LibreOffice at 25 as a free office suite that somehow qualifies for the paid chart (likely a nominal listing fee).

iOS App Store Top 20 Paid

Rank App Category
1 HotSchedules Business/Scheduling
2 Shadowrocket VPN/Proxy
3 Procreate Pocket Art/Drawing
4 AnkiMobile Flashcards Education
5 Paprika Recipe Manager 3 Lifestyle
6 SkyView Education/Astronomy
7 TonalEnergy Tuner & Metronome Music
8 AutoSleep Health/Sleep
9 Forest: Focus for Productivity Productivity
10 RadarScope Weather
11 Monash FODMAP Diet Health
12 Merge Watermelon for watch Game/Watch
13 Streaks Habit Tracking
14 Wipr 2 Safari Ad Block
15 µBrowser Watch Utility
16 PeakFinder Outdoors
17 Threema Secure Messaging
18 Things 3 Task Management
19 Goblin Tools AI Productivity
20 iVerify Basic Security

iOS tells a different story from Mac. HotSchedules (employee scheduling for restaurants) tops the chart — a niche B2B app with zero consumer brand recognition quietly printing money. The health/wellness cluster is striking: AutoSleep, Monash FODMAP Diet, Forest, and Streaks collectively represent the "pay for self-improvement" buyer psychology unique to iPhone. Goblin Tools at rank 19 is the only explicitly AI-powered app on either chart — a collection of AI tools for neurodivergent users. It's a signal that AI-native consumer apps are just beginning to crack the paid charts.

Cross-Platform Winners

Apps appearing on both Mac and iOS top paid charts:

App Mac Rank iOS Rank Cross-Platform Signal
Shadowrocket 1 2 Peak dominance — VPN proxy utility
Wipr 2 5 14 Safari ad blocking across devices
RadarScope 19 10 Professional weather — niche but loyal
Things 3 17 18 Premium task management — consistent demand

Shadowrocket is the undisputed cross-platform champion. A single-purpose proxy utility app sitting at #1 Mac and #2 iOS demonstrates the enormous demand for network tools in the current privacy/VPN era — particularly in markets where internet censorship drives proxy adoption. The four cross-platform winners share a trait: they solve one problem exceptionally well and charge a one-time fee.

Money vs Hype Gap

Hyped but not monetizing (directly): The entire AI coding agent ecosystem (8/10 Trendshift entries) generates massive attention but almost no direct consumer revenue. DeepSeek is VC-funded. The .claude skills repos are open-source. Agent frameworks are open-source. The monetization path is indirect — developer tools, consulting, and enterprise licensing — which explains why none of these appear on any paid app chart.

Quietly printing money: HotSchedules (#1 iOS paid, restaurant scheduling), AnkiMobile (#4 iOS, flashcards at $24.99), Paprika Recipe Manager (#5 iOS). None of these will ever trend on GitHub or Product Hunt. They serve non-technical professionals who happily pay for focused, reliable tools. This is the "boring app" revenue thesis in action.

Key Patterns

  1. Agent skills are the new packages. The .claude directory ecosystem (217K+ combined stars) signals that AI agent configuration — prompt templates, tool definitions, workflow rules — is becoming a first-class development artifact, as significant as npm packages or Docker configs were in their respective eras.

  2. Safari extensions are the stealth revenue channel. With 4 entries on the Mac top 25 (Wipr 2, Noir, Dark Reader, Tampermonkey), Safari extensions represent the most commercially viable indie macOS category. Low development complexity, recurring demand, and Apple's native distribution make this the lowest-friction path to paid app revenue on any platform.

  3. Health/wellness dominates iOS paid but is absent on Mac. Forest, Streaks, AutoSleep, Monash FODMAP — the iPhone's always-with-you form factor creates a unique willingness to pay for self-improvement tools that simply doesn't exist on desktop.

  4. TrimRx's 27% growth is the strongest SaaS signal. GLP-1 telehealth is a structural trend, not a fad. Digital-first distribution of weight loss medication is creating a new SaaS category with healthcare margins and tech scaling.

Quick Takes

The gap between what's trending (AI agent tools) and what's making money (utility apps, niche B2B, telehealth) has never been wider. The opportunity is in bridging it — building focused, paid utility apps using the AI agent tools that dominate GitHub trending. The developer who ships a $4.99 Mac utility built with agentic coding workflows captures both worlds.
